app.name: REMS
app.tagline: Office equipment repair, maintenance & replacement tracking

landing.hero_subtitle: Automate servicing of printers, copiers, computers and other office equipment. Transparency, control, efficiency.
landing.features_title: Everything you need for equipment management
landing.features_subtitle: A comprehensive solution for managing repair and maintenance requests
landing.f1_desc: Centralised logging of all issues with printers, MFPs, computers and other office equipment.
landing.f2_desc: Monitor work progress in real time from request submission to closure.
landing.f3_desc: Maintain an asset registry with service history, warranty dates and specifications.
landing.f4_desc: Role separation: owner and employees. Task assignment and progress tracking inside the organisation.
landing.f5_desc: Instant alerts on status changes, technician assignments and completed work.
landing.f6_desc: Statistics on requests, completion times, workload and equipment health.
landing.roles_subtitle: REMS supports different access levels within a single organisation
landing.role_owner_desc: Creates the organisation, adds staff, oversees all requests and sees full statistics.
landing.role_manager_desc: Accepts and assigns requests to technicians, monitors deadlines.
landing.role_tech_desc: Receives tasks, records completed work and comments on repair progress.
landing.role_emp_desc: Submits repair requests for their equipment and tracks request status.
landing.flow_title: From request to closure — three steps
landing.flow_subtitle: A simple, clear process for all participants
landing.flow1_desc: Describes the fault, specifies the equipment and attaches photos if needed.
landing.flow2_desc: The request enters the queue; the manager assesses priority and assigns a specialist.
landing.flow3_desc: The specialist records the outcome; the employee receives a closure notification.
landing.guide_section_sub: REMS works equally well for managing repairs within a single company and for collaboration between two organisations.
landing.guideA1_desc: Signs up as Owner — the system creates the organisation and provides a unique ID.
landing.guideA2_desc: Employees sign up with the org ID. The owner approves join requests.
landing.guideA3_desc: One employee files a request → another picks it up and closes it after the repair. The owner sees the whole flow.
landing.guideB_tag: Client and contractor in one system
landing.guideB1_desc: Your company (client) and your service partner (contractor) create separate organisations in REMS.
landing.guideB2_desc: The contractor becomes your service partner — you get a single channel for submitting repair requests.
landing.guideB3_desc: Your staff create requests → contractor's employees pick them up → you track every stage in real time.
landing.statuses_subtitle: Every request follows a clear path from submission to closure
landing.status_new_desc: The request has been submitted by an employee and is awaiting acceptance.
landing.status_accepted_desc: The request has been accepted; priority and timing have been assessed.
landing.status_inprogress_desc: The assignee is carrying out repair or diagnosis of the equipment.
landing.status_done_desc: The assignee has recorded the result and is awaiting confirmation.
landing.status_closed_desc: The request is closed. All work has been completed and confirmed.
landing.cta_subtitle: Join organisations already using REMS for maintenance management.
landing.footer: © 2026 REMS — Office Equipment Repair Request Management System
